description: Address security and correctness issues found in review: add authentication middleware, change default bind to 127.0.0.1, fix CPU usage always 0%, add input validation on settings, replace blocking I/O with async.
steps: Add password field to DashboardConfig (empty string default = no auth required, same as RCON pattern)
Change default bind address from 0.0.0.0:8080 to 127.0.0.1:8080
Add authentication middleware/extractor for /api/ routes that checks Bearer token matching configured password (skip if password is empty)
Store sysinfo::System in AppState (wrapped in Mutex) and refresh on each /api/status call for accurate CPU readings
Add input validation in put_settings: validate default_gamemode and default_difficulty against known valid values, return 400 if invalid
Replace std::fs::read_to_string and std::fs::write with tokio::fs equivalents in put_settings handler
Add comment explaining actix-web runtime compatibility with tokio
acceptance_criteria: DashboardConfig has a password field with empty default
API routes require Bearer token when password is non-empty
CPU usage is read from a persistent sysinfo::System instance
Invalid gamemode/difficulty values are rejected with 400
File I/O in put_settings uses tokio::fs
verification: Run 'cargo check -p pumpkin-config' and confirm 0 errors
Run 'cargo check -p pumpkin' and confirm 0 errors (pre-existing WASM errors excluded)
Run 'cargo clippy -p pumpkin-config' and confirm no clippy errors
